mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects expanding consumer spending that drives higher corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending represents the majority of US GDP
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market where rising wage costs compress corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and a federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 1.25% indicate that tightening monetary policy is intercepting strong consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative mean 5-day returns in walk-forward testing, or the interaction term adds no significant explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond the individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter decline in the real GDP growth rate (GDPC1) signals fading aggregate demand that forces downward revisions to corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth slows during late-cycle expansions, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,100 thousand units demonstrates durable residential investment demand that lifts construction-related earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following HOUST > 1100 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or occur less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization that supports near-term earnings surprises, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative mean 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the hit ratio falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signal an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy faster than priced, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Month-over-month increase in both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL does not produce negative mean 20-day returns, or a linear regression interaction term has a p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 1.25% confirms the central bank has moved decisively away from the zero lower bound, increasing the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.25%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
